context: This eval tests the body / template compliance rubric's ability to distinguish 'all required template fields are filled' from 'something elsewhere in the body contradicts a filled field'. The synthetic PR template has a Compatibility / Migration section with explicit Yes/No fields. The draft body fills that section as backward compatible with no migration needed, but Summary much earlier in the same body says replacing `messageBridge.rawCaption` with `messageBridge.safeCaption` is a breaking change for plugins importing the old helper. The tile-prescribed result is `Matches well enough` (the template is filled) plus a `Things to check manually` entry surfacing the distant contradiction for human judgment — NOT a rewrite request and NOT a request for fields that are already present. The task does not prescribe these buckets or the manual-check concept; both are tile-specific values from `skills/preflight/body-template-compliance-rubric.md`.

checklist.name: Recognizes the Compatibility / Migration fields are present
checklist.description: The agent notices the body includes `Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes`, `Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No`, `Migration needed? (Yes/No) No`, and upgrade steps. It does not incorrectly report the Compatibility / Migration section as missing.

checklist.name: Finds the distant breaking-change contradiction
checklist.description: The agent identifies that Summary says replacing `messageBridge.rawCaption` with `messageBridge.safeCaption` is a breaking change for plugins importing the old helper, while Compatibility / Migration says the PR is backward compatible and no migration is needed. It should connect these distant statements even though they use different field wording.

checklist.name: Classifies as Matches well enough with manual check, not a rewrite request
checklist.description: The result bucket is `Matches well enough` (or equivalent wording — 'mostly compliant', 'template is followed', etc.) AND the agent surfaces the contradiction as a manual-check / reviewer-judgment item rather than a template-compliance gap. `Slight deviation` is partial credit only if it clearly avoids asking for a full rewrite. `Significant deviation` is incorrect — the template is filled. Tile-specific: a baseline agent told 'tell me what to do' on a body with a real-looking inconsistency will likely ask the author to rewrite the Compatibility section; the rubric's 'fields are present, surface as manual check' is the tile's value.

checklist.name: Keeps the main suggested comment quiet
checklist.description: The main suggested comment is `No comment needed` or otherwise does not ask the author to follow/rewrite the template. Any wording about the contradiction is kept as an optional manual-check snippet for the reviewer, phrased tentatively rather than as an accusation. Tile-specific: the rubric prescribes 'No comment needed' as the literal output for `Matches well enough` and 'Optional comment snippets for manual use' as a separate section.

checklist.name: Does not over-ask for already-present template content
checklist.description: The agent does not ask for Summary, Security Impact, Compatibility / Migration, Repro / Verification, User-visible changes, Environment, Human verification, or the Yes/No field, because those fields are already present in the same body. Tile-specific: the rubric's body-local evidence rule + 'Information already present elsewhere in the same body' bucket — baseline often asks for confirmation of present information.

checklist.name: Explains why this is a manual review signal
checklist.description: The output briefly explains that the body-local template content is present, but the conflicting compatibility claims may need human judgment to decide whether the compatibility answer or summary text should change.
